title: trifolium resupinatum subsp. clusii
intro: Trifolium resupinatum subsp. clusii, also known as the small or small-flowered Persian clover, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family Fabaceae. It has been found to have medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which contribute to its use in traditional medicine. The health benefits associated with Trifolium resupinatum subsp. clusii include its potential to aid in the management of diabetes and its use as a natural remedy for digestive issues. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and dried extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine practices.
